name: debug fastethernet
detail: Enable debugging of the Ethernet management port.

params.name: af
params.description: Displays Ethernet management port software-address-filter debug messages. events Displays Ethernet management port event debug messages. packets Displays Ethernet management port packet debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug fastethernet { af | events | packets} command is the same as the no debug fastethernet{af| events | packets} command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on a member switch, you can start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number EXEC command.

name: debug ilpower
detail: Enable debugging of the power controller and Power over Ethernet (PoE) system.
syntax: debug ilpower {cdp | controller | event | ha | port | powerman | registries | scp | sense}
noForm: no debug ilpower {cdp | controller | event | ha | port | powerman | registries | scp | sense}

params.name: cdp
params.description: Displays PoE Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) debug messages. controller Displays PoE controller debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: Displays PoE event debug messages.

params.name: ha
params.description: Displays PoE high-availability messages.

params.name: port
params.description: Displays PoE port manager debug messages. powerman Displays PoE power management debug messages. registries Displays PoE registries debug messages.

params.name: scp
params.description: Displays PoE SCP debug messages.

params.name: sense
params.description: Displays PoE sense debug messages.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on PoE-capable switches.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on a member switch, you can start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number EXEC command.

name: debug interface
detail: Enable debugging of interface-related activities.
syntax: debug interface {interface-id | counters {exceptions | protocol memory} | null interface-number | port-channel port-channel-number | states|vlan vlan-id} | port-channel port-channel-number | states|vlan vlan-id}
noForm: no debug interface {interface-id | counters {exceptions | protocol memory} | null interface-number

params.name: interface-id
params.description: ID of the physical interface. Displays debug messages for the specified physical port, identified by type switch number/module number/port, for example, gigabitethernet 1/0/2.

params.name: null interface-number
params.description: Displays debug messages for null interfaces. The interface number is always 0.

params.name: port-channel
params.description: Displays debug messages for the specified EtherChannel port-channel

params.name: port-channel-number
params.description: interface. The port-channel-number range is 1 to 48.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: Displays debug messages for the specified VLAN. The vlan range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays counters debugging information.

params.name: exceptions
params.description: Displays debug messages when a recoverable exceptional condition occurs during the computation of the interface packet and data rate statistics.

params.name: protocol memory
params.description: Displays debug messages for memory operations of protocol counters.

params.name: states
params.description: Displays intermediary debug messages when an interface's state transitions.

usageSummary: If you do not specify a keyword, all debug messages appear.  The undebug interface command is the same as the no debug interface command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.

name: debug lldp packets
detail: Enable debugging of Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) packets.
usageSummary: The undebug lldp packets command is the same as the no debug lldp packets command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the .  To enable debugging on a member switch, you can start a session from the by using the session switch-number EXEC command.

name: debug nmsp
detail: Enable debugging of the Network Mobility Services Protocol (NMSP) on the switch.

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all NMSP debug messages.

params.name: connection
params.description: Displays debug messages for NMSP connection events.

params.name: error
params.description: Displays debugging information for NMSP error messages.

params.name: event
params.description: Displays debug messages for NMSP events.

params.name: rx
params.description: Displays debugging information for NMSP receive messages.

params.name: tx
params.description: Displays debugging information for NMSP transmit messages.

params.name: packet
params.description: Displays debug messages for NMSP packet events.

usageSummary: Note Attachment information is not supported in Cisco IOS XE Denali 16. 1. 1 and later releases.

name: duplex
detail: Specify the duplex mode of operation for a port.
usageSummary: For Fast Ethernet ports, setting the port to auto has the same effect as specifying half if the attached device does not autonegotiate the duplex parameter.  For Gigabit Ethernet ports, setting the port to auto has the same effect as specifying full if the attached device does not autonegotiate the duplex parameter.

name: errdisable detect cause
detail: Enable error-disable detection for a specific cause or for all causes.
syntax: errdisable detect cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard shutdown vlan | dhcp-rate-limit | dtp-flap | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | pagp-flap | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psp shutdown vlan | security-violation shutdown vlan | sfp-config-mismatch} | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | pagp-flap | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psp shutdown vlan | security-violation shutdown vlan | sfp-config-mismatch}
noForm: no errdisable detect cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard shutdown vlan | dhcp-rate-limit | dtp-flap

params.name: all
params.description: Enables error detection for all error-disabled causes.

params.name: arp-inspection
params.description: Enables error detection for dynamic Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inspection.

params.name: bpduguard shutdown vlan
params.description: Enables per-VLAN error-disable for BPDU guard.

params.name: dhcp-rate-limit
params.description: Enables error detection for DHCP snooping.

params.name: dtp-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for the Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) flapping.

params.name: gbic-invalid
params.description: Enables error detection for an invalid Gigabit Interface Converter (GBIC) module.

params.name: Note
params.description: This error refers to an invalid small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module.

params.name: inline-power
params.description: Enables error detection for the Power over Ethernet (PoE) error-disabled cause.

params.name: Note
params.description: This keyword is supported only on switches with PoE ports.

params.name: link-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for link-state flapping.

params.name: loopback
params.description: Enables error detection for detected loopbacks.

params.name: pagp-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) flap error-disabled cause.

params.name: pppoe-ia-rate-limit
params.description: Enables error detection for the PPPoE Intermediate Agent rate-limit error-disabled cause.

params.name: psp shutdown vlan
params.description: Enables error detection for protocol storm protection (PSP).

params.name: security-violation shutdown
params.description: Enables voice aware 802.1x security. vlan

params.name: sfp-config-mismatch
params.description: Enables error detection on an SFP configuration mismatch. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 58 Interface and Hardware errdisable detect cause

usageSummary: A cause (such as a link-flap or dhcp-rate-limit) is the reason for the error-disabled state.  When a cause is detected on an interface, the interface is placed in an error-disabled state, an operational state that is similar to a link-down state.  When a port is error-disabled, it is effectively shut down, and no traffic is sent or received on the port.

name: errdisable detect cause small-frame
detail: Allow any switch port to be error disabled if incoming VLAN-tagged packets are small frames (67 bytes or less) and arrive at the minimum configured rate (the threshold).
usageSummary: This command globally enables the small-frame arrival feature.  Use the small violation-rate interface configuration command to set the threshold for each port.  You can configure the port to be automatically re-enabled by using the errdisable recovery cause small-frame global configuration command.

name: errdisable recovery cause
detail: Enable the error-disabled mechanism to recover from a specific cause.
syntax: errdisable recovery cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard | channel-misconfig | dhcp-rate-limit | dtp-flap | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | mac-limit | pagp-flap | port-mode-failure | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psecure-violation | psp | security-violation | sfp-config-mismatch | storm-control | udld | vmps} | dtp-flap | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | mac-limit | pagp-flap | port-mode-failure | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psecure-violation | psp | security-violation | sfp-config-mismatch | storm-control | udld | vmps}
noForm: no errdisable recovery cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard | channel-misconfig | dhcp-rate-limit

params.name: all
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from all error-disabled causes.

params.name: arp-inspection
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inspection error-disabled state.

params.name: bpduguard
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) guard error-disabled state.

params.name: channel-misconfig
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the EtherChannel misconfiguration error-disabled state.

params.name: dhcp-rate-limit
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the DHCP snooping error-disabled state.

params.name: dtp-flap
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) flap error-disabled state.

params.name: gbic-invalid
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from an invalid Gigabit Interface Converter (GBIC) module error-disabled state.

params.name: Note
params.description: This error refers to an invalid small form-factor pluggable (SFP) error-disabled state.

params.name: inline-power
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the Power over Ethernet (PoE) error-disabled state. This keyword is supported only on switches with PoE ports.

params.name: link-flap
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the link-flap error-disabled state.

params.name: loopback
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from a loopback error-disabled state.

params.name: mac-limit
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the mac limit error-disabled state.

params.name: pagp-flap
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP)-flap error-disabled state. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 61 Interface and Hardware errdisable recovery cause

params.name: port-mode-failure
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the port mode change failure error-disabled state.

params.name: pppoe-ia-rate-limit
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the PPPoE IA rate limit error-disabled state.

params.name: psecure-violation
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from a port security violation disable state.

params.name: psp
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the protocol storm protection (PSP) error-disabled state.

params.name: security-violation
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from an IEEE 802.1x-violation disabled state.

params.name: sfp-config-mismatch
params.description: Enables error detection on an SFP configuration mismatch.

params.name: storm-control
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from a storm control error.

params.name: udld
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) error-disabled state.

params.name: vmps
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the VLAN Membership Policy Server (VMPS) error-disabled state.

usageSummary: A cause (such as all or BDPU guard) is defined as the reason that the error-disabled state occurred.  When a cause is detected on an interface, the interface is placed in the error-disabled state, an operational state similar to link-down state.  When a port is error-disabled, it is effectively shut down, and no traffic is sent or received on the port.

name: errdisable recovery cause small-frame
detail: Use the errdisable recovery cause small-frame global configuration command on the switch to enable the recovery timer for ports to be automatically re-enabled after they are error disabled by the arrival of small frames. Use the no form of this command to return to the default setting.
usageSummary: This command enables the recovery timer for error-disabled ports.  You configure the recovery time by using the errdisable recovery interval interface configuration command.  This example shows how to set the recovery timer: Device(config)# errdisable recovery cause small-frame Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: errdisable recovery interval
detail: Specify the time to recover from an error-disabled state.
usageSummary: The error-disabled recovery timer is initialized at a random differential from the configured interval value.  The difference between the actual timeout value and the configured value can be up to 15 percent of the configured interval.  You can verify your settings by entering the show errdisable recovery privileged EXEC command.

name: lldp
detail: Enable Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) on an interface.

params.name: med-tlv-select
params.description: Selects an LLDP Media Endpoint Discovery (MED) time-length-value (TLV) element to send.

params.name: tlv
params.description: String that identifies the TLV element. Valid values are the following: • inventory-management— LLDP MED Inventory Management TLV. • location— LLDP MED Location TLV. • network-policy— LLDP MED Network Policy TLV.

params.name: receive
params.description: Enables the interface to receive LLDP transmissions.

params.name: tlv-select
params.description: Selects the LLDP TLVs to send.

params.name: power-management
params.description: Sends the LLDP Power Management TLV.

params.name: transmit
params.description: Enables LLDP transmission on the interface.

usageSummary: This command is supported on 802. 1 media types.  If the interface is configured as a tunnel port, LLDP is automatically disabled.

name: mdix auto
detail: Enable the automatic medium-dependent interface crossover (auto-MDIX) feature on the interface.
usageSummary: When auto-MDIX is enabled, the interface automatically detects the required cable connection type (straight-through or crossover) and configures the connection appropriately.  When you enable auto-MDIX on an interface, you must also set the interface speed and duplex to auto so that the feature operates correctly.  When auto-MDIX (and autonegotiation of speed and duplex) is enabled on one or both of the connected interfaces, link up occurs, even if the cable type (straight-through or crossover) is incorrect.

name: network-policy
detail: Apply a network-policy profile to an interface.

params.name: profile-number
params.description: The network-policy profile number to apply to the interface.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile number interface configuration command to apply a profile to an interface.  You cannot apply the switchport voice vlan command on an interface if you first configure a network-policy profile on it.  However, if switchport voice vlan vlan-id is already configured on the interface, you can apply a network-policy profile on the interface.

name: network-policy profile
detail: Create a network-policy profile and to enter network-policy configuration mode.

params.name: profile-number
params.description: Network-policy profile number. The range is 1 to 4294967295.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  To return to privileged EXEC mode from the network-policy profile configuration mode, enter the exit command.  When you are in network-policy profile configuration mode, you can create the profile for voice and voice signaling by specifying the values for VLAN, class of service (CoS), differentiated services code point (DSCP), and tagging mode.

name: nmsp attachment suppress
detail: Suppress the reporting of attachment information from a specified interface.
usageSummary: Use the nmsp attachment suppress interface configuration command to configure an interface to not send location and attachment notifications to a Cisco Mobility Services Engine (MSE).

name: power efficient-ethernet auto
detail: Enable Energy Efficient Ethernet (EEE) for an interface.
usageSummary: You can enable EEE on devices that support low power idle (LPI) mode.  Such devices can save power by entering LPI mode during periods of low utilization.  In LPI mode, systems on both ends of the link can save power by shutting down certain services.

name: power inline
detail: Configure the power management mode on Power over Ethernet (PoE) ports.
syntax: power inline {auto [max max-wattage] | never | port priority {high | low} | static [max max-wattage]}
noForm: no power inline {auto | never | port priority {high | low} | static [max max-wattage]}

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables powered-device detection. If enough power is available, automatically allocates power to the PoE port after device detection. Allocation is first-come, first-serve.

params.name: max max-wattage
params.description: (Optional) Limits the power allowed on the port. The range is 4000 to 30000 mW. If no value is specified, the maximum is allowed.

params.name: never
params.description: Disables device detection, and disables power to the port.

params.name: port
params.description: Configures the power priority of the port. The default priority is low.

params.name: priority {high|low}
params.description: Sets the power priority of the port. In case of a power supply failure, ports configured as low priority are turned off first and ports configured as high priority are turned off last. The default priority is low.

params.name: static
params.description: Enables powered-device detection. Pre-allocates (reserves) power for a port before the switch discovers the powered device. This action guarantees that the device connected to the interface receives enough power.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on PoE-capable ports.  If you enter this command on a port that does not support PoE, this error message appears: Device(config)# interface gigabitethernet1/0/1 Device(config-if)# power inline auto ^ % Invalid input detected at '^' marker.  In a switch stack, this command is supported on all ports in the stack that support PoE.

name: power inline consumption
detail: Override the amount of power specified by the IEEE classification for a powered device.
usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.  When Cisco powered devices are connected to PoE ports, the switch uses Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) to determine the CDP-specific power consumption of the devices, which is the amount of power to allocate based on the CDP messages.  The switch adjusts the power budget accordingly.

name: power inline police
detail: Enable policing of real-time power consumption on a powered device.

params.name: action
params.description: (Optional) Configures the device to turn off power to the port if the real-time power

params.name: errdisable
params.description: consumption exceeds the maximum power allocation on the port. This is the default action.

params.name: action log
params.description: (Optional) Configures the device to generate a syslog message while still providing power to a connected device if the real-time power consumption exceeds the maximum power allocation on the port.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.  This command is supported only on Power over Ethernet (PoE)-capable ports.  If you enter this command on a device or port that does not support PoE, an error message appears.

name: show eee
detail: Display Energy Efficient Ethernet (EEE) information for an interface.
syntax: show eee{counters | capabilities interface interface-id | status interface interface-id}

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays EEE counters.

params.name: capabilities
params.description: Displays EEE capabilities for the specified interface.

params.name: status
params.description: Displays EEE status information for the specified interface.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: Specifies the interface for which to display EEE capabilities or status information.

usageSummary: You can enable EEE on devices that support low power idle (LPI) mode.  Such devices can save power by entering LPI mode during periods of low power utilization.  In LPI mode, systems on both ends of the link can save power by shutting down certain services.

name: show env
detail: Display fan, temperature, redundant power system (RPS) availability, and power information.
syntax: show env {all | fan | power [{all | switch [stack-member-number]}] | rps | stack [stack-member-number] | temperature [status]}

params.name: all
params.description: Displays the fan and temperature environmental status and the status of the internal power supplies and the RPS.

params.name: fan
params.description: Displays the switch fan status.

params.name: power
params.description: Displays the internal power status of the active switch.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays the status of all the internal power supplies in a standalone switch when the command is entered on the switch, or in all the member switches when the command is entered on the active switch.

params.name: switch
params.description: (Optional) Displays the status of the internal power supplies for each switch in the stack or for the specified switch. This keyword is available only on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: stack-member-number
params.description: (Optional) Number of the member switch for which to display the status of the internal power supplies or the environmental status. The range is 1 to 8.

params.name: rps
params.description: Displays the RPS status.

params.name: stack
params.description: Displays all environmental status for each switch in the stack or for the specified switch. This keyword is available only on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: temperature
params.description: Displays the switch temperature status.

params.name: status
params.description: (Optional) Displays the switch internal temperature (not the external temperature) and the threshold values.

usageSummary: Use the show env EXEC command to display the information for the switch being accessed—a standalone switch or the active switch.  Use this command with the stack and switch keywords to display all information for the stack or for the specified member switch.  If you enter the show env temperature status command, the command output shows the switch temperature state and the threshold level.

name: show errdisable detect
detail: Display error-disabled detection status.
usageSummary: A gbic-invalid error reason refers to an invalid small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module.  The error-disable reasons in the command output are listed in alphabetical order.  The mode column shows how error-disable is configured for each feature.

name: show errdisable recovery
detail: Display the error-disabled recovery timer information.
usageSummary: A gbic-invalid error-disable reason refers to an invalid small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module interface.

name: show interfaces
detail: Display the administrative and operational status of all interfaces or for a specified interface.
syntax: show interfaces [{interface-id | vlan vlan-id}] [{accounting | capabilities [module number] | debounce | description | etherchannel | flowcontrol | pruning | stats | status [{err-disabled}] | trunk}]

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports (including type, stack member for stacking-capable switches, module, and port number) and port channels. The port channel range is 1 to 48.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) VLAN identification. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: accounting
params.description: (Optional) Displays accounting information on the interface, including active protocols and input and output packets and octets.

params.name: Note
params.description: The display shows only packets processed in software; hardware-switched packets do not appear.

params.name: capabilities
params.description: (Optional) Displays the capabilities of all interfaces or the specified interface, including the features and options that you can configure on the interface. Though visible in the command line help, this option is not available for VLAN IDs.

params.name: module number
params.description: (Optional) Displays capabilities of all interfaces on the switch or specified stack member. The range is 1 to 8. This option is not available if you entered a specific interface ID.

params.name: debounce
params.description: (Optional) Displays port debounce timer information for an interface.

params.name: description
params.description: (Optional) Displays the administrative status and description set for an interface.

params.name: etherchannel
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface EtherChannel information.

params.name: flowcontrol
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface flow control information.

params.name: mtu
params.description: (Optional) Displays the MTU for each interface or for the specified interface.

params.name: pruning
params.description: (Optional) Displays trunk VTP pruning information for the interface.

params.name: stats
params.description: (Optional) Displays the input and output packets by switching the path for the interface. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 87 Interface and Hardware show interfaces

params.name: status
params.description: (Optional) Displays the status of the interface. A status of unsupported in the Type field means that a non-Cisco small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module is inserted in the module slot.

params.name: err-disabled
params.description: (Optional) Displays interfaces in an error-disabled state.

params.name: trunk
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface trunk information. If you do not specify an interface, only information for active trunking ports appears.

params.name: Note
params.description: Though visible in the command-line help strings, the crb, fair-queue, irb, mac-accounting, precedence, random-detect, and rate-limit keywords are not supported.

usageSummary: The show interfaces capabilities command with different keywords has these results: • Use the show interface capabilities module number command to display the capabilities of all interfaces on that switch in the stack.  If there is no switch with that module number in the stack, there is no output.  • Use the show interfaces interface-id capabilities to display the capabilities of the specified interface.

name: show interfaces counters
detail: Display various counters for the switch or for a specific interface.
syntax: show interfaces [interface-id] counters [{errors | etherchannel | module stack-member-number | protocol status | trunk}]

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the physical interface, including type, stack member (stacking-capable switches only) module, and port number.

params.name: errors
params.description: (Optional) Displays error counters.

params.name: etherchannel
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel counters, including octets, broadcast packets, multicast packets, and unicast packets received and sent.

params.name: module
params.description: (Optional) Displays counters for the specified stack member. stack-member-number The range is 1 to 8.

params.name: Note
params.description: In this command, the module keyword refers to the stack member number. The module number that is part of the interface ID is always zero.

params.name: protocol status
params.description: (Optional) Displays the status of protocols enabled on interfaces.

params.name: trunk
params.description: (Optional) Displays trunk counters.

params.name: Note
params.description: Though visible in the command-line help string, the vlan vlan-id keyword is not supported.

usageSummary: If you do not enter any keywords, all counters for all interfaces are included.  This is an example of partial output from the show interfaces counters command.  It displays all counters for the switch.

name: show interfaces switchport
detail: Display the administrative and operational status of a switching (nonrouting) port, including port blocking and port protection settings.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports (including type, stack member for stacking-capable switches, module, and port number) and port channels. The port channel range is 1 to 48.

params.name: backup
params.description: (Optional) Displays Flex Link backup interface configuration for the specified interface or all interfaces.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed backup information for the specified interface or all interfaces on the switch or the stack. module number (Optional) Displays switchport configuration of all interfaces on the switch or specified stack member. The range is 1 to 8. This option is not available if you entered a specific interface ID.

usageSummary: Use the show interface switchport module number command to display the switch port characteristics of all interfaces on that switch in the stack.  If there is no switch with that module number in the stack, there is no output.  This is an example of output from the show interfaces switchport command for a port.

name: show interfaces transceiver
detail: Display the physical properties of a small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module interface.
syntax: show interfaces [interface-id] transceiver [{detail | module number | properties | supported-list | threshold-table}]

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the physical interface, including type, stack member (stacking-capable switches only) module, and port number.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays calibration properties, including high and low numbers and any alarm information for any Digital Optical Monitoring (DoM)-capable transceiver if one is installed in the switch.

params.name: module number
params.description: (Optional) Limits display to interfaces on module on the switch. The range is 1 to 8. This option is not available if you entered a specific interface ID.

params.name: properties
params.description: (Optional) Displays speed, duplex, and inline power settings on an interface.

params.name: supported-list
params.description: (Optional) Lists all supported transceivers. threshold-table (Optional) Displays alarm and warning threshold table.

name: show ip ports all
detail: Display all the open ports on the device.

name: show network-policy profile
detail: Display the network-policy profiles.

name: show power inline
detail: Display the Power over Ethernet (PoE) status for the specified PoE port, the specified stack member, or for all PoE ports in the switch stack.
syntax: show power inline [{police | priority}] [{interface-id | module stack-member-number}] [detail]

params.name: police
params.description: (Optional) Displays the power policing information about real-time power consumption.

params.name: priority
params.description: (Optional) Displays the power inline port priority for each port.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the physical interface.

params.name: module stack-member-number
params.description: (Optional) Limits the display to ports on the specified stack member. The range is 1 to 8. This keyword is supported only on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed output of the interface or module.

name: show system mtu
detail: Display the global maximum transmission unit (MTU) or maximum packet size set for the switch.
usageSummary: For information about the MTU values and the stack configurations that affect the MTU values, see the system mtu command.

name: speed
detail: Specify the speed of a 10/100/1000/2500/5000 Mbps port.

params.description: Specifies that the port runs at 10 Mbps.
Specifies that the port runs at 100 Mbps.
Specifies that the port runs at 1000 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on 10/100/1000 Mb/s ports.
Specifies that the port runs at 2500 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on multi-Gigabit-supported Ethernet ports.
Specifies that the port runs at 5000 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on multi-Gigabit-supported Ethernet ports.

params.name: auto
params.description: Detects the speed at which the port should run, automatically, based on the port at the other end of the link. If you use the 10, 100, 1000, 1000, 2500, or 5000 keyword with the auto keyword, the port autonegotiates only at the specified speeds. nonegotiate Disables autonegotiation, and the port runs at 1000 Mbps.

usageSummary: You cannot configure speed on 10-Gigabit Ethernet ports.  Except for the 1000BASE-T small form-factor pluggable (SFP) modules, you can configure the speed to not negotiate (nonegotiate) when an SFP module port is connected to a device that does not support autonegotiation.  The new keywords, 2500 and 5000 are visible only on multi-Gigabit (m-Gig) Ethernet supporting devices.

name: switchport backup interface
detail: Configure Flex Links.
syntax: switchport backup interface interface-id [{mmu primary vlan vlan-id | multicast fast-convergence | preemption {delay seconds | mode {bandwidth | forced | off}} | prefer vlan vlan-id}] preemption {delay | mode} | prefer vlan}]
noForm: no switchport backup interface interface-id [{mmu primary vlan | multicast fast-convergence |

params.name: interface-id
params.description: ID of the physical interface.

params.name: mmu
params.description: (Optional) Configures the MAC move update (MMU) for a backup interface pair.

params.name: primary vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) VLAN ID of the primary VLAN. The range is 1 to 4094. multicast fast-convergence (Optional) Configures multicast fast convergence on the backup interface.

params.name: preemption
params.description: (Optional) Configures a preemption scheme for a backup interface pair.

params.name: delay seconds
params.description: Specifies a preemption delay. The range is 1 to 300 seconds. The default is 35 seconds.

params.name: mode
params.description: Specifies the preemption mode.

params.name: bandwidth
params.description: Specifies that a higher bandwidth interface is preferred.

params.name: forced
params.description: Specifies that an active interface is preferred.

params.name: off
params.description: Specifies that no preemption occurs from backup to active.

params.name: prefer vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that VLANs are carried on the backup interfaces of a Flex Link pair. VLAN ID range is 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: Flex Links are a pair of interfaces that provide backup to each other.  With Flex Links configured, one link acts as the primary interface and forwards traffic, while the other interface is in standby mode, ready to begin forwarding traffic if the primary link shuts down.  The interface being configured is referred to as the active link; the specified interface is identified as the backup link.

name: switchport block
detail: Prevent unknown multicast or unicast packets from being forwarded.

params.name: Note
params.description: Only pure Layer 2 multicast traffic is blocked. Multicast packets that contain IPv4 or IPv6 information in the header are not blocked.

params.name: unicast
params.description: Specifies that unknown unicast traffic should be blocked.

usageSummary: By default, all traffic with unknown MAC addresses is sent to all ports.  You can block unknown multicast or unicast traffic on protected or nonprotected ports.  If unknown multicast or unicast traffic is not blocked on a protected port, there could be security issues.

name: system mtu
detail: system mtu {bytes | jumbo bytes} no system mtu

params.name: bytes
params.description: Set the system MTU for ports that are set to 10 or 100 Mb/s. The range is 1500 to 1998 bytes. This is the maximum MTU received at 10/100-Mb/s Ethernet switch ports.

params.name: jumbo
params.description: Set the system jumbo MTU for Gigabit Ethernet ports operating at 1000 Mb/s or greater. The

params.name: bytes
params.description: range is 1500 to 9000 bytes. This is the maximum MTU received at the physical port for Gigabit Ethernet ports.

usageSummary: The switch does not support the MTU on a per-interface basis.  When you use this command to change the system MTU or jumbo MTU size, you must reset the switch before the new configuration takes effect.  The system MTU setting is saved in the switch environmental variable in NVRAM and becomes effective when the switch reloads.

name: voice-signaling vlan
detail: Create a network-policy profile for the voice-signaling application type.
syntax: voice-signaling vlan {vlan-id [{cos cos-value | dscp dscp-value}] | dot1p [{cos l2-priority | dscp dscp}] | none | untagged}

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) The VLAN for voice traffic. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: cos cos-value
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Layer 2 priority class of service (CoS) for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 7; the default is 5. dscp dscp-value (Optional) Specifies the differentiated services code point (DSCP) value for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 63; the default is 46.

params.name: dot1p
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to use IEEE 802.1p priority tagging and to use VLAN 0 (the native VLAN).

params.name: none
params.description: (Optional) Does not instruct the Cisco IP phone about the voice VLAN. The phone uses the configuration from the phone key pad.

params.name: untagged
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to send untagged voice traffic. This is the default for the phone.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  The voice-signaling application type is for network topologies that require a different policy for voice signaling than for voice media.  This application type should not be advertised if all of the same network policies apply as those advertised in the voice policy TLV.

name: voice vlan
detail: Create a network-policy profile for the voice application type.
syntax: voice vlan {vlan-id [{cos cos-value | dscp dscp-value}] | dot1p [{cos l2-priority | dscp dscp}] | none | untagged}

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) The VLAN for voice traffic. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: cos cos-value
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Layer 2 priority class of service (CoS) for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 7; the default is 5. dscp dscp-value (Optional) Specifies the differentiated services code point (DSCP) value for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 63; the default is 46.

params.name: dot1p
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to use IEEE 802.1p priority tagging and to use VLAN 0 (the native VLAN).

params.name: none
params.description: (Optional) Does not instruct the Cisco IP phone about the voice VLAN. The phone uses the configuration from the phone key pad.

params.name: untagged
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to send untagged voice traffic. This is the default for the phone.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  The voice application type is for dedicated IP telephones and similar devices that support interactive voice services.  These devices are typically deployed on a separate VLAN for ease of deployment and enhanced security through isolation from data applications.
